feed.title: blank
feed.subtitle: A simple, whitespace theme for academics. Based on [*folio](https://github.com/bogoli/-folio) design.

entry.title: PaperclipOffice
entry.content:
PaperclipOffice is a 2D Pixel-Art Electron Desktop Application built with TypeScript and React, designed to visualize a Paperclip AI company as a living, interactive office building.

Instead of traditional dashboards, the system turns real-time data from a Paperclip instance—such as agents, projects, issues, costs, and approvals—into animated entities that move, interact, and evolve across a multi-floor pixel-art environment. Each floor represents different layers of activity, creating a continuous, game-like simulation of organizational flow.

Alongside the visual simulation, React-based panels provide structured insights including metrics, live activity streams, and drill-down modals. This hybrid approach allows users to both see the system behavior intuitively and inspect detailed operational data when needed.

Check here for details.
entry.summary: Pixel-art simulation of a Paperclip AI company in real time

entry.title: CronyClaw
entry.content:
CronyClaw is a Vibe Coding Web & Desktop Application built with TypeScript, powered by a Python backend, and packaged with Electron for a seamless cross-platform desktop experience.

Inspired by and extending the original OpenLLM Vtuber, the project brings to life an interactive Live2D AI character capable of real-time conversation, voice cloning, emotional expression, and contextual screen awareness. The system integrates multiple LLM and VLM technologies (such as Qwen, OpenAI, Gemini, and Ollama) alongside TTS/ASR pipelines to enable rich, multimodal interaction.

CronyClaw also features deep integration with OpenClaw via OpenClaw Bridge, allowing the AI to connect through direct Auth Integration or modular Skill-based execution. This enables the assistant to manage tasks (e.g., creating, starting, completing workflows) through a unified interface.

Users can interact using text or voice (ASR), while the AI dynamically composes and executes skills to perform complex actions, effectively turning the character into a capable, autonomous task operator.

Check here for more details.
entry.summary: AI-powered Live2D assistant with multimodal interaction and OpenClaw integration

entry.title: AI Art Portal for Game Development

link.title: AI Art Portal for Game Development

entry.content:
AI Art Portal for Game Development is a Vibe Coding Web Application built with TypeScript on the frontend and a Python backend, designed to streamline and scale AI-driven content creation for game development.

The platform integrates advanced models and tools such as Nano Banana, Qwen Editing, and Wan 2.6, enabling users to generate and refine both images and videos through standardized, reusable workflows. Rather than isolated prompts, it focuses on building structured pipelines that improve consistency, speed, and iteration across asset production.

At its core, the system acts as a centralized hub for managing AI workflows—allowing teams to organize, reuse, and evolve creative processes efficiently. It is particularly suited for rapid prototyping, asset generation, and experimentation in game pipelines.

Additionally, the platform supports MCP (Model Context Protocol), enabling seamless integration with tools like Claude and Cursor. This allows developers and artists to automate tasks, chain workflows, and scale production using familiar AI-assisted environments.

Check here for details.
entry.summary: AI-powered portal for scalable game asset creation workflows

entry.title: BrainHub
entry.content:
BrainHub is an AI-powered marketing platform built to help Vietnamese users create, optimize, and scale their marketing efforts through accessible and practical AI tools.

The platform provides a suite of free AI tools designed for real-world use cases—ranging from content creation and optimization to campaign ideation and automation. Instead of focusing purely on technology, BrainHub emphasizes usability and real impact for marketers, creators, and small businesses.

Beyond tools, BrainHub also serves as a knowledge hub, offering educational resources and a blog system written in Markdown. These materials are designed to guide users in understanding and applying AI effectively in everyday marketing workflows, bridging the gap between theory and practice.

BrainHub

The platform also includes a dedicated tool portal, where users can directly access and experiment with various AI-powered utilities in a streamlined interface.

BrainHub Tools

Check here for details.
entry.summary: AI-powered marketing platform for Vietnamese users

entry.title: AI Game Warehouse Application
entry.content:
AI Game Warehouse Application is a Vibe Coding Web Application built with TypeScript and powered by Google Gemini, designed as a hub for AI-generated interactive game experiences.

The platform acts as a repository of playable AI-generated games, where each entry is a self-contained application that runs directly in the browser. Beyond static gameplay, users can interact with AI in real time within each game, enabling dynamic narratives, adaptive mechanics, and more engaging experiences.

Rather than focusing on polished releases, the system is built for rapid prototyping and experimentation—making it easy to create, store, and showcase new ideas. It provides a lightweight environment for testing AI-driven gameplay concepts, iterating quickly, and sharing demos without complex setup.

Check here for details.
entry.summary: Platform for hosting and exploring AI-generated game prototypes

entry.title: AI Character in Game
entry.content:
AI Character in Game is part of a Vibe Coding Game series, exploring how AI-driven characters can enhance gameplay through interaction, personality, and dynamic behavior.

The 2D prototype is developed using Pygame, inspired by the original Shooter game, but extended with real-time AI interaction. The in-game character—designed, generated, and animated via PixelLab—features a distinct pixel-art style and is powered by Qwen (LLM + TTS), enabling natural dialogue, voice output, and more immersive player engagement.

The project also extends into a 3D prototype, where characters are generated using the Hunyuan 3D platform from Tencent. This version builds on the Multiplayer Shooter Game, integrating AI-driven characters into a real-time multiplayer environment.

By combining AI-generated assets with language and voice capabilities, the project demonstrates how intelligent characters can move beyond scripted behavior to become interactive, expressive elements within both 2D and 3D game worlds.

Check here for details.
entry.summary: AI-driven characters integrated into 2D and 3D game prototypes

entry.title: Fast Game Asset Generation Application
entry.content:
Fast Game Asset Generation Application is a Vibe Coding Web Application built with TypeScript on the frontend and a Python backend, focused on ultra-fast AI-powered image generation for game development.

The system leverages cutting-edge models such as Sana, capable of generating images in under 0.2 seconds per image, and Infinity for scalable, high-quality generation. This combination enables near real-time asset creation, making it ideal for rapid iteration workflows.

Beyond speed, the platform provides advanced capabilities including ControlNet for guided generation, Inpainting for precise editing, and custom LoRA training to adapt and personalize styles. These features allow users to not only generate assets quickly, but also refine and control outputs with a high degree of flexibility.

Designed for developers and artists, the system supports a wide range of use cases—from stickers and sprites to textures and visual assets—streamlining the asset creation pipeline for games and interactive applications.

Check here for details.
entry.summary: High-performance AI system for rapid game asset generation

entry.title: Multi-scale Twin-attention for 3D Instance Segmentation
entry.content:
Visualize results on validation set of ScanNetv2 dataset.

Top row: from left to right are Input Point Cloud, High-scale Superpoint, Low-scale Superpoint and Ground-truth respectively
Bottom row: from left to right are ISBNet, SPFormer, MAFT and MSTA3D respectively

Other examples

1 / 4
ISBNet
2 / 4
SPFormer
3 / 4
MAFT
4 / 4
MSTA3D
❮ ❯
entry.summary: Visualization and comparison of 3D point cloud instance segmentation results

entry.title: NeuralNetwork Application for Mobile Robot
entry.content:
NeuralNetwork Application for Mobile Robot is a project focused on applying neural network techniques to real-world robotic navigation and control.

The system demonstrates a mobile robot following a predefined path (black line) using learned behaviors, showcasing stable and responsive movement in physical environments. This highlights the practical application of neural networks in low-level control and perception tasks.

Real-world experiments of the robot navigating different track configurations.

In addition to physical experiments, the project includes simulation of an obstacle restriction method using Webots. These simulations explore how the robot can perceive, avoid obstacles, and adapt its trajectory in a controlled 3D environment.

By combining real-world experiments with simulation, the project provides a comprehensive view of how neural networks can be applied to robot navigation, control systems, and obstacle avoidance.

Check here for details.
entry.summary: Neural network-based control for mobile robot navigation and obstacle avoidance

entry.title: 3DoF Arm Robot Simulation
entry.content:
3DoF Arm Robot Simulation is a project focused on modeling and controlling a robotic arm using SolidWorks for mechanical design and MATLAB for simulation and control.

The system demonstrates a 3 Degrees of Freedom (3DoF) robotic arm capable of executing precise movements such as writing trajectories and following straight-line paths. These simulations highlight fundamental concepts in kinematics, trajectory planning, and control systems.

Check here for details.

In addition, the project extends to a grasping robot simulation, exploring object manipulation and interaction in a virtual environment. This includes simulating how the robot approaches, grips, and handles objects using coordinated joint control.

Check here for details.

Overall, the project provides a foundation in robotic arm simulation, combining mechanical design with control algorithms to study motion accuracy and manipulation tasks.
entry.summary: Simulation of robotic arm control and grasping using SolidWorks and MATLAB
